    - button "Context window 21% full, 41.6K of 200K tokens used" [ref=e68]
    - button "More actions" [ref=e69]
    - button "Artifacts" [ref=e70]
    - paragraph: I wanted to see if you could help look into an issue that I think has been caused by a recent change, maybe around caching. Right now a lot of the sessions that I go to aren't loading the full history, or even more than just a few messages. The path below is one that is doing that. /Users/adam/.middleman/profiles/middleman-project/sessions/review-upstream
    - text: "[Web]"
    - paragraph: 10:33 AM
    - paragraph: Looking into this — I'll investigate the session history loading issue. Let me examine the session data and the recent caching-related code changes to find the root cause.
    - text: "[Web] 10:33 AM"
    - button "Copy message" [ref=e71]
    - button "Thumbs up" [ref=e72] [nth=1]
    - button "Thumbs down" [ref=e73] [nth=1]
    - button "Add comment" [ref=e74] [nth=1]
    - paragraph: "Found the root cause. Here's the chain:"
    - list:
      - listitem:
        - strong: Conversation cache fills to 2,000 entries
        - text: (the
        - code: MAX_CONVERSATION_HISTORY
        - text: cap), mostly
        - code: agent_tool_call
        - text: entries with large JSON payloads — in
        - code: review-upstream
        - text: that's 8.2 MB of tool calls vs just 1.1 MB of actual conversation messages.
      - listitem:
        - strong: Bootstrap WS byte budget is ~1 MB
        - text: (
        - code: MAX_WS_EVENT_BYTES - 16KB
        - text: ). When the UI subscribes to a session,
        - code: selectBootstrapConversationHistory
        - text: tries to send all 2,000 entries as one WS event, sees it's ~9.4 MB, then binary-searches for the latest N entries that fit in 1 MB.
      - listitem:
        - strong: Tool calls crowd out conversation messages.
        - text: Since tool calls and messages are interleaved chronologically, the tail-trim keeps only ~200 recent entries total, of which maybe a handful are actual visible
        - code: conversation_message
        - text: entries.
    - paragraph:
      - text: "Multiple sessions are affected:"
      - code: mobile-app
      - text: (43 MB cache!),
      - code: middleman-project
      - text: (9.2 MB),
      - code: review-upstream
      - text: (9.3 MB),
      - code: slash-commands
      - text: (2.3 MB).
    - paragraph:
      - strong: "The fix:"
      - code: selectBootstrapConversationHistory
      - text: needs to prioritize conversation messages over activity entries when fitting within the byte budget — strip tool calls first, not conversation messages. I'm spawning a worker to implement this now.
    - text: "[Web] 10:36 AM"
    - button "Copy message" [ref=e75] [nth=1]
    - button "Thumbs up" [ref=e76] [nth=2]
    - button "Thumbs down" [ref=e77] [nth=2]
    - button "Add comment" [ref=e78] [nth=2]
    - paragraph:
      - text: "Fix is implemented and validated. Single file change:"
      - button "ws-handler.ts /Users/adam/repos/middleman/apps/backend/src/ws/ws-handler.ts" [ref=e79]
      - text: (+133/-32 lines).
    - paragraph:
      - strong: "What changed:"
      - code: selectBootstrapConversationHistory
      - text: "now uses a two-phase budget fitting strategy instead of a naive tail-trim:"
    - list:
      - listitem:
        - strong: Conversation-first priority
        - text: — all
        - code: conversation_message
        - text: and
        - code: conversation_log
        - text: entries (the actual user-visible transcript) are included first.
      - listitem:
        - strong: Activity entries fill remaining budget
        - text: —
        - code: agent_tool_call
        - text: and
        - code: agent_message
        - text: entries are added from the tail until the ~1 MB WS budget is full.
      - listitem:
        - strong: Graceful degradation
        - text: — if even conversation messages alone exceed the budget, it falls back to tail-trimming just those entries.
      - listitem:
        - strong: Chronological order preserved
        - text: — the merge step filters the original source array by a Set of selected entries, maintaining order.
    - paragraph:
      - strong: "Before:"
      - code: review-upstream
      - text: had 9.4 MB of mixed entries → binary search keeps a tiny tail → user sees ~5 messages.
      - strong: "After:"
      - text: 1.1 MB of conversation messages all fit within budget → all 128 visible messages load, plus recent activity entries fill the remaining space.
    - paragraph:
      - text: Typechecks pass on both backend and UI. The two test failures (
      - code: index-shutdown-signals
      - text: and
      - code: ws-server
      - text: PID file) are pre-existing on
      - code: main
      - text: — confirmed by running tests with the change stashed.
    - paragraph: Ready for a restart to pick up the fix, or I can commit it first if you want.
    - text: "[Web] 10:42 AM"
    - button "Copy message" [ref=e80] [nth=2]
    - button "Thumbs up" [ref=e81] [nth=3]
    - button "Thumbs down" [ref=e82] [nth=3]
